sourceMovie.title: Thor: Ragnarok
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Thor: Ragnarok (2017) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Taika Waititi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Avengers: Infinity War (2018) captures the action appeal of Thor: Ragnarok without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Avengers: Infinity War at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Thor: Ragnarok — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Guardians of the Galaxy (2014). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Captain America: Civil War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Captain America: Civil War (2016) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Thor: Ragnarok rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Black Panther
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Black Panther (2018) is the kind of film Thor: Ragnarok fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 shares Thor: Ragnarok's gift for action — the 2017 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Iron Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Thor: Ragnarok compelling — the action, the adventure — shows up in Iron Man 2 (2010) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Thor: Ragnarok is alive in Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Captain Marvel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Captain Marvel (2019) operates in the same action as Thor: Ragnarok, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Venom: Let There Be Carnage (2021) belongs to the same conversation as Thor: Ragnarok — both anchor their action in adventure emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Eternals
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thor: Ragnarok's action stayed with you, Eternals (2021) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The adventure through-line is the real common thread between them.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thor: Ragnarok?
faq.answer: Try Avengers: Infinity War, Guardians of the Galaxy, Captain America: Civil War—each shares DNA with Thor: Ragnarok while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thor: Ragnarok?
faq.answer: Thor: Ragnarok (2017) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thor: Ragnarok part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Thor Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
